anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Blow-up scenarios**

(a) **Runaway loops:** 20 timers discovered but zero projected calls — auditor isn't parsing them. If any timer calls Anthropic/Gemini directly (bypassing `audit_check`), no cap applies. A 60s cron looping Opus = ~$2,880/day.

(b) **Missing caps:** Anthropic cap relies on a `/tmp` lockfile — wiped on reboot/container restart, allowing multiple "first runs/day." No per-hour or per-month ceiling. Gemini cap is per-process; multiple workers = N×100/day.

(c) **2× rate:** Gemini → 200 calls/day (still cheap). Anthropic → lockfile race condition could mean $4–20/day silently.

(d) **Most likely silent burn:** **Gemini via `gemma4_helper`** — cheap-per-call, 100/day headroom, no dollar alarm, and any new caller inherits the limit invisibly. Add hard USD caps and parse the
ai_consensus.grok:
**Blow-up risks:**

(a) **Runaway loops**: 20 timers + zero projected calls suggests audit may miss retry loops or new trading-logic timers that hammer Anthropic/Gemini on errors, bypassing the lockfile.

(b) **Missing daily caps**: Gemini cap only guards two audit functions; any new trading signal or backtest caller bypasses it entirely. Anthropic Haiku warning already flags reintroduction risk.

(c) **2x rate**: Anthropic jumps from $0 to $4/day (lockfile failure). Negligible vs Gemini.

(d) **Stealth burn king**: Anthropic. Lockfile on /tmp fails in containers/restarts; $2 Opus calls can fire multiple times/day with no visible log or hard cap. Operator sees nothing until bill arrives.

**Recommendation**: Add centralized rate-limiter before bot goes live. (98 words)
